It is a little greedy to spend energy on that, but still full points, of course. 22...Qc6 Black has two other ways to try to defend. a) 22...Qe7 deprives the king of its only square: 23.Qxa4+ is mating. b) 22...Rf8 23.Qxe6+ Be7 24.Rxf8+ Kxf8 ✓ 25.Rd7 wins the queen with a winning material advantage, or 25.Rf1+ with a winning attack: 25...Ke8 26.Qf7+ Kd7 27.Rd1+ Kc6 28.Qe6+ Kc5 29.Rd7+– 23.Qxg7 Rf8 24.Rxf8+ Bxf8 25.Qxh7+– ✓ Black has no active moves, and no defence against 26.Qg6+ Ke7 27.Rd6 Qc8 28.Qf6+ Ke8 29.Rxe6+. This is the essential element that cannot be measured by any analysis or device, and I believe it's at the heart of success in all things: the power of intuition and the ability to harness and use it like a master.
